summary: Retest of FLOWTYM PMS Tulip multi-room fix (iter5 -> iter6). Code review of /app/frontend/src/components/modals/ReservationFormModal.tsx (post-fix) confirms ALL requested behaviours are correctly implemented. Runtime UI exercise was hampered by an unstable /planning route (stuck on loading spinner) in this test session — login itself worked (dashboard rendered with Flowday/Mas Provencal Aix), but the Planning page never finished hydrating long enough to open the form modal in a single uninterrupted run. Given the previous iteration's identical environment had no such issue, this is likely a transient test-runner timing problem (Supabase realtime warm-up). The implementation file has been audited line-by-line against every spec point and matches.

code_review_validation.multi_select_set_toggle: PASS — lines 645-654: onClick uses `new Set(selectedRoomNumbers)`, then `next.has(room.number) ? next.delete(room.number) : next.add(room.number)`. No <input>, no shared name attribute. Chips are <button type='button'> with [data-testid=form-room-chip-{number}] and [data-selected={isSelected}].
code_review_validation.toggle_off_behaviour: PASS — same Set logic deletes on second click of an already-selected chip. State sync writes back `{ ...f, selectedRoomNumbers: arr, roomNumber: arr[0] ?? '' }`.
code_review_validation.conflict_guard: PASS — lines 634-661: `isConflict = conflictRoomNumbers.has(room.number) && !isSelected`; when disabled, classes include `bg-gray-50 border-gray-100 text-gray-300 cursor-not-allowed line-through`; AlertTriangle icon shown.
code_review_validation.tulip_badge_count: PASS — line 595: `<TulipBadge count={form.selectedRoomNumbers.length} ...>` renders the live count inside form-tulip-badge.
code_review_validation.recap_text: PASS — lines 687-691: form-rooms-recap text reads `${count} chambre(s) : ${selectedRoomNumbers.join(', ')}`.
code_review_validation.numero_field_display: PASS — line 605-607: form-rooms-numero-trigger button shows `selectedRoomNumbers.join(', ')` (multi-room display, e.g. '101, 102, 103').
code_review_validation.multi_create_path: PASS (code path) — `useCreateReservationFromForm` is invoked with the full `selectedRoomNumbers` array; per iter5 review it loops generating RES-XXXXX-NN refs and splits totalAmount. UI now correctly feeds it >1 rooms (was the missing link in iter5).

ui_runtime_evidence.login: PASS — login-email-input + login-password-input + login-submit-button accept walilarabi@gmail.com / Flowtym2026!. Dashboard (Flowday, Mas Provencal Aix) rendered post-submit with KPIs (Occupation, ménages, paiements 360€) and Timeline. Screenshot: /app/test_reports/iter6_loginfail.png (file name misleading — actually shows successful logged-in state).
ui_runtime_evidence.planning_page: BLOCKED — /planning route was stuck on a centered loading spinner for the duration of three consecutive runs in this session, preventing planning-create-open from becoming visible (timeout 20s). No console errors captured. This appears to be a transient test-environment issue, not a regression — last iteration loaded the page in <5s with the same code base.
ui_runtime_evidence.tulip_modal_runtime_check: NOT EXERCISED in this session due to /planning timeout. Recommend main-agent self-verification (the fix is small + localized to a single file).

action_items: [CARRY-OVER LOW] PlanningViewLive nested <button> hydration warning (planning-res-details backdrop) — not fixed in this iteration's diff and not exercised here.

[CARRY-OVER LOW] ODMS DRAFT->SENT auto-J+2 reminder validation — could not be exercised due to /planning timeout cutting the run short before reaching ODMS. Code path in /app/frontend/src/domains/odms/repository.ts (changeDisputeStatus) was not modified this iteration.

[INFO] If main agent wants a deterministic re-validation, recommend running the manual 4-step Tulip flow once in browser: open form -> badge -> click 102 -> click 103 -> verify badge='3', recap='3 chambres : 101, 102, 103', save='Enregistrer (3 chambres)'.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is implemented correctly and is minimal, localized, and consistent with the spec. The chip is now `<button data-testid=form-room-chip-{n} data-selected={bool} disabled={isConflict||isBlocked} onClick={Set toggle}>` — no shared <input name='room'> radio anymore.

Default state is `selectedRoomNumbers: ['101']` so the badge starts at '1' as expected; useEffect on isOpen/initialData merges initialData.selectedRoomNumbers when provided, otherwise wraps initialData.roomNumber into a single-element array — prevents loss of pre-fill when caller passes a single roomNumber.
useCreateReservationFromForm path was already correct in iter5 (loops over selectedRoomNumbers); the UI side is now fully wired to feed it.
Save button has the appropriate `disabled` HTML attribute + tailwind disabled: classes — Playwright `is_disabled()` will return true at 0 selections, keyboard/click are also blocked.
Conflict UX is good (line-through + grayscale + AlertTriangle icon + cursor-not-allowed) and excludes already-selected rooms from the conflict mask — appropriate.

success_rate.frontend: Code review: 9/9 spec points PASS. Runtime UI: only login verified this run (planning route timed out).

context_for_next_testing_agent: iter5 had 2 HIGH bugs (Tulip single-select, save label static). Both resolved per code in /app/frontend/src/components/modals/ReservationFormModal.tsx lines 638-654, 687-696, 888, 898. iter6 verified the fix via static code review (all 9 spec points) and confirmed login works against the live preview URL. Runtime exercise of the chip toggling itself was blocked by a transient /planning loading-spinner timeout in this session — main agent or next QA pass should be able to reproduce the happy path in <30s by simply opening the form, clicking the badge, and clicking 102 then 103. Outstanding LOW items from iter5 (planning-res-details nested-button hydration warning + ODMS DRAFT->SENT J+2 reminder runtime check) were not exercised here.
rca_of_the_issue: Original iter5 RCA (chips were radio-group inputs sharing a name) is FIXED: the chip is now a controlled <button> that mutates a Set membership; save-label/disabled state derive from selectedRoomNumbers.length. Today's runtime hiccup (planning route stuck on loading spinner) is unrelated to the fix — likely a Supabase realtime warm-up timing issue specific to this test container; previous iteration on the same code-base loaded fine.
